summary_en: KOYOSHA raised FY2026 year-end dividend forecast from ¥7.20 to ¥10.00 per share (post-5:1 split) following better-than-expected earnings driven by carbon offset printing sales.
title_en: Notice of Revision of Consolidated Full-Year Earnings Forecast and Dividend Forecast (Increased Dividend) for Fiscal Year Ending March 31, 2026
translation_en_brief: KOYOSHA INC. (7946, TSE Standard/Nagoya Main) issued notice on May 19, 2026 of both revised full-year earnings and increased dividend forecast for fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Full-year consolidated results exceeded prior guidance: sales ¥4.83 billion (+1.7%), operating profit ¥102 million (+46.3%), and net income ¥84 million (+29.4%). The company attributes outperformance primarily to stronger-than-expected carbon offset printing sales (carbon-neutral and carbon-zero print solutions). The company revised its dividend forecast upward by ¥2.80 per share: year-end dividend now projected at ¥10.00 per share (previously ¥7.20), implying a 30% payout ratio. The company maintains a dividend policy targeting stable distributions based on annual business results while reinforcing internal reserves for future business expansion. Note: A 5:1 stock split executed on March 8, 2026 affects per-share figures reported herein.
